description: Has every schema contained in the document — every specified operation input/output, every entry in schemas, and their subschemas as JSON Schema 2020-12 defines them — well-formed: valid against the JSON Schema 2020-12 meta-schemas and satisfying the constraints of §5.2. Verification uses locally available meta-schemas and MUST NOT require a network fetch. The rule does not require proving satisfiability, resolving external resources, or rejecting unknown keywords.
notes: Schema positions are operation input, operation output, and entries in the top-level schemas map; subschemas are covered recursively as JSON Schema 2020-12 defines them, so one meta-schema validation of each top-level position covers its whole subtree. 0.2.0 admits boolean schemas at every schema position (§5.2: true accepts every value, false accepts none, {} is equivalent to true), so the positive cases exercise boolean form at top-level and nested positions alongside object form. The rule is deliberately narrow: the positives include an unparseable `pattern` regular expression and an unresolvable external $ref, both of which pass the meta-schemas per the rule text and surface only when the schema is used. Negative cases list OBI-D-17 alone even where the derived document schema would also reject the value structurally, per this corpus's OBI-D-02 suppression convention.

tests.description: object schemas with nested subschemas at operation input and output are well-formed
boolean schema true at operation input is a well-formed schema (contract specified, every value satisfies it)
boolean schema false at operation output is a well-formed schema (contract specified, no value satisfies it)
empty object schema {} at operation input is well-formed (the equivalent spelling of true)
boolean schema as an entry in the top-level schemas map is well-formed
boolean schemas at nested subschema positions (properties value, additionalProperties, items) are well-formed
unknown keywords are legitimate annotations, not well-formedness violations
an unparseable pattern regular expression passes the meta-schemas (operability of keyword values is not established by this rule)
an unresolvable external $ref passes: well-formedness does not require resolving external resources
type keyword with a number value at operation input is not a schema in the pinned dialect
type keyword naming an unknown simple type fails the meta-schemas
a string at operation output is neither object nor boolean form
a number as an entry in the top-level schemas map is neither object nor boolean form
a keyword value of the wrong JSON type at a nested subschema position (minLength as a string) fails recursively
required as a string instead of an array inside an items subschema at operation input
oneOf as an object instead of an array at operation output
a number as a subschema inside an anyOf array is neither object nor boolean form
